evals.prompt: I have a fresh Obsidian vault at evals/fixtures/fresh-vault and want to set up the full Karpathy-style workflow for AI engineering notes. Create the right folders and schema files so future agents know how to maintain it, and document any optional repo/entity extensions without forcing them on.
evals.expected_output: Routes to kb-init, creates the raw/wiki/outputs contract, documents immutable raw guidance, and explains optional repo/entity support as opt-in.
expectations: The workflow chooses kb-init rather than compile, query, or health.
The setup includes raw, wiki, outputs, AGENTS.md, and CLAUDE.md.
The response preserves the idea that raw notes are immutable.
The response treats raw/repos and wiki/entities as optional rather than mandatory.

evals.prompt: Use evals/fixtures/compiled-vault as the target vault. I clipped a new article, updated an older one, and dropped a repo companion note. Compile the wiki without touching the raw notes themselves.
evals.expected_output: Routes to kb-compile, detects new or changed sources, updates summaries and concepts, optionally updates entities if the evidence is strong enough, refreshes indices, and keeps raw files unchanged.
expectations: The workflow chooses kb-compile.
The compile pass uses summary metadata such as source_mtime or source_hash.
The response reports new, changed, and skipped counts.
The response reports concept and entity updates separately rather than conflating them.

evals.prompt: Using evals/fixtures/compiled-vault, what do my notes say about retrieval-augmented generation versus markdown indices? Save the answer if it is substantive, and log the query if you archive it.
evals.expected_output: Routes to kb-query in research mode, checks existing Q&A first, cites concept and summary pages, archives the answer, and notes the query-log write-back.
expectations: The workflow chooses kb-query.
The query pass checks outputs/qa before re-deriving the answer.
The answer includes provenance and an archive-by-default posture.
The response mentions appending a query entry to wiki/log.md when the answer is substantive.

evals.prompt: Take evals/fixtures/compiled-vault and turn the current knowledge into a short X thread about why people should not jump straight to RAG for personal knowledge bases.
evals.expected_output: Routes to kb-query in publish mode, uses prior Q&A or creates supporting Q&A first, saves a grounded thread draft, and records the publish step in the log.
expectations: The workflow uses publish mode rather than only research mode.
The output stays grounded in wiki pages or prior Q&A.
The workflow identifies where the thread draft should be saved.
The workflow identifies that a publish entry should be appended to wiki/log.md for a substantive artifact.

evals.prompt: Run a weekly health check on evals/fixtures/drift-vault. I care about contradictions, orphan notes, stale claims, alias drift, missing cross-links, and whether I should still stay markdown-first or upgrade search.
evals.expected_output: Routes to kb-health, scores the vault across the health rubric, calls out stale Q&A and alias drift, and gives a search upgrade recommendation.
expectations: The workflow chooses kb-health.
The report includes Completeness, Consistency, Connectivity, Freshness, and Provenance scores.
The report calls out alias drift or duplicated concepts.
The report includes a search upgrade recommendation instead of assuming RAG by default.

evals.prompt: My Obsidian wiki at evals/fixtures/drift-vault feels disconnected and outdated after a month of new reading. Which workflow step should I run first and why?
evals.expected_output: Routes through the package entry skill, identifies the vault as a health-first case, and explains the recommendation.
expectations: The package entry skill makes a lifecycle diagnosis rather than blindly compiling.
The recommendation prefers kb-health because the symptom is compiled-layer drift.
The answer explains the assumption behind the routing decision.

evals.prompt: My Notion workspace is messy and I want a tagging strategy for product notes. Should I rebuild the database or use relation properties?
evals.expected_output: Does not force the Obsidian Karpathy workflow onto a Notion-specific problem.
expectations: The response recognizes that this is not an Obsidian knowledge-base workflow request.
The workflow avoids pretending kb-init, kb-compile, kb-query, or kb-health are the right tools.

evals.prompt: I need help designing a vector search service for my SaaS docs with embeddings, reranking, and a retrieval API. This is not an Obsidian vault.
evals.expected_output: Does not over-trigger on generic knowledge-base language and should avoid forcing the markdown-wiki workflow.
expectations: The response recognizes a search infrastructure request rather than an Obsidian vault workflow.
The workflow does not default to this skill just because RAG or knowledge base was mentioned.

evals.prompt: Use evals/fixtures/compiled-vault. I dropped a repo note at raw/repos/2026-04-05-qmd-local-search.md about qmd. Compile it and decide whether qmd should stay a concept or get an entity page, without rewriting the raw note.
evals.expected_output: Routes to kb-compile, accepts raw/repos as a valid input class, preserves raw immutability, and uses a durable-entity threshold instead of blindly creating an entity page for every named tool.
expectations: The workflow treats raw/repos as an optional but valid source location.
The response preserves the idea that raw notes are immutable.
The response treats entity-page creation as conditional on durable evidence rather than mandatory.

evals.prompt: Use evals/fixtures/root-raw-vault. The vault is already initialized, but I dumped two markdown notes directly under raw/ and never created a CLAUDE.md. Compile the new sources into the wiki without rewriting the raw files.
evals.expected_output: Routes to kb-compile, accepts root-level raw notes, treats missing CLAUDE.md as non-blocking, and reports the missing optional file rather than failing.
expectations: The package entry diagnoses this as a compile case rather than an init case.
The compile pass accepts markdown files directly under raw/ as valid sources.
The workflow does not treat missing CLAUDE.md as blocking.
The response preserves raw immutability and surfaces the missing optional file in the summary.

evals.prompt: Inspect evals/fixtures/partial-vault. It has some KB folders already, but the setup is incomplete and I want the workflow repaired before I start compiling new notes. What should run first?
evals.expected_output: Routes through the package entry skill to kb-init in repair mode because the vault is only partially initialized and the support layer is incomplete.
expectations: The package entry diagnoses a partial-init or repair case rather than compile, query, or health.
The recommendation prefers kb-init because the support layer is incomplete.
The answer calls out missing contract files or directories as the decisive signal.

evals.prompt: Run a KB health pass on evals/fixtures/broken-render-vault. The index table looks broken in Obsidian and I want lint-and-repair behavior, not just a vague warning.
evals.expected_output: Routes to kb-health, detects alias-style wikilinks inside table cells, and treats the issue as a safe mechanical repair when the target note is clear.
expectations: The workflow chooses kb-health.
The health pass explicitly flags alias-style wikilinks inside Markdown table cells.
The remediation posture treats the table rewrite as a safe mechanical fix when the target is clear.
The response distinguishes render integrity from a generic search-upgrade recommendation.

evals.prompt: Use evals/fixtures/pdf-only-vault. There is a paper PDF under raw/papers/ and an adjacent .source.md sidecar with paper metadata. Compile the vault without touching raw/. Treat any PDF under raw/papers/ as a paper that must go through paper-workbench in json mode, use the sidecar only as metadata, and report the missing install step if paper-workbench is unavailable.
evals.expected_output: Routes to kb-compile, treats the PDF as a valid paper source, still records the sidecar-derived paper handle as metadata, always prefers paper-workbench json mode for raw/papers PDFs, and reports missing companion skills without pretending the PDF compiled successfully when paper-workbench is absent.
expectations: The workflow chooses kb-compile.
The compile pass treats raw/papers/*.pdf as valid source material.
The response uses the sidecar or filename only as metadata for the paper PDF, not as the routing gate.
The response says to skip only the affected PDF and surface install guidance when paper-workbench is missing.

evals.prompt: Inspect evals/fixtures/missing-agents-vault. The vault already has raw/wiki/outputs structure, but somebody deleted the local AGENTS.md contract file. What should run first before I let anyone compile or query it again?
evals.expected_output: Routes through the package entry skill to kb-init in repair mode because the required local AGENTS.md contract is missing, even though the rest of the vault is structurally present.
expectations: The package entry diagnoses a repair case rather than compile, query, or health.
The recommendation explicitly cites missing AGENTS.md as the decisive signal.
The response does not treat missing only CLAUDE.md as equivalent to missing AGENTS.md.

evals.prompt: Use evals/fixtures/pdf-no-handle-vault. There is a paper PDF under raw/papers/, but there is no arXiv handle in a sidecar or the filename. Compile the vault without touching raw/, still route it through paper-workbench in json mode because the directory means it is a paper, and do not fall back to pdf if paper-workbench is unavailable.
evals.expected_output: Routes to kb-compile, treats the PDF as a valid paper source, uses paper-workbench json mode even without a deterministic handle because raw/papers is the routing signal, and skips with install guidance instead of falling back to pdf when paper-workbench is unavailable.
expectations: The workflow chooses kb-compile.
The compile pass does not try to infer a paper identity from title text alone.
The response still routes through paper-workbench when no deterministic paper handle exists.
